description_long: This bird has a slender, streamlined body with a long, slightly curved beak, ideal for probing flowers or crevices. Its plumage is primarily a muted greenish-brown on the upper body, blending into grayish and brown tones along the wings and underparts. The wings are narrow and pointed, lying flat against the body, while the tail is straight and tapers to a subtle point. The legs are short and not prominently visible, suggesting a perching or hovering species. The surface of the feathers appears matte with a soft texture, and the overall form is delicate and lightweight. This bird would be well-suited to urban parks, gardens, or areas with flowering plants.
description: A slender bird with a long, curved beak, muted greenish-brown plumage, and a pointed tail. Its streamlined body and matte feathers suggest a delicate, lightweight species.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's long, curved beak, streamlined body, and subtle greenish-brown plumage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the narrow head, beak alignment, and the symmetry of the wings and tail.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the elongated beak and the smooth transition of colors along the body.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tapering tail and the subtle gradient of color from the back to the tail tip.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid with subtle gradients and slight mottling on the wings and back
